segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens on a vibrant blue rink, bustling with an NHL All-Star mascot competition, surrounded by cheering spectators and photographers. Upbeat, energetic background music plays as the announcer introduces the first mascot, a white and black character performing a backflip. The crowd's excitement builds as the announcer proclaims, "Our newest mascot from the great Northwest, the Seattle Kraken!" leading to the grand entrance of Buoy, a blue-haired, fish-like character in a teal jersey, sliding and dancing energetically to loud cheers. Next, the announcer highlights a mascot who "never passes on a moment to show off his Stanley Cup ring," introducing Chance from the Vegas Golden Knights, a yellow and black dog-like mascot who performs cartwheels and high-kicks, with the announcer remarking, "Look at that ring, that's a big one." The segment concludes with Sabretooth from the Buffalo Sabres, a green and yellow saber-toothed tiger, sliding and waving, as the announcer begins his introduction, calling him "the cat from Buffalo."

An announcer's voice introduces "Carlton," the "Eastern Conference Captain," to enthusiastic crowd cheers and upbeat background music, setting a celebratory tone. A large group of mascots, including Gritty (an orange monster in a Flyers jersey), Sparky (a blue fox in an Islanders jersey), Blades (a brown bear in a Bruins jersey), Spartacat (an orange-haired cat in a Senators jersey), Youppi! (an orange bear in a Canadiens jersey), Thunderbug (a blue bug in a Lightning jersey), and Stanley C. Panther (a tan panther in a Panthers jersey), gather in a tight huddle at center ice. They are seen kneeling, sitting, and interacting playfully, some waving or gesturing to the visibly engaged crowd behind the barriers. segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens on a vibrant, blue indoor rink branded with 'COLLECT THE BEST' and 'All-Star' logos, set within a bustling convention hall filled with cheering spectators. An announcer humorously remarks, "we might need that after the game... Just in case of all the flu we got down here," followed by a whistle. He then comically struggles with timekeeping, asking, "Alright, who's keeping... I keeping time? Who's in? I keeping time? I got the time," before declaring a "10 minute one o'clock period." The game kicks off with the announcer's "Ready? Go!", as mascots in NHL jerseys, including Louie (St. Louis Blues), Bernie (Colorado Avalanche), and Harvey the Hound (Calgary Flames) near the goal, and Nordy (Minnesota Wild), Fin (Vancouver Canucks), and Victor E. Green (Dallas Stars) scattered across the rink, begin their playful, non-competitive hockey action. A referee mascot oversees the fun. The announcer notes, "Chester Cheetah has dropped the ball," quickly correcting himself, "Let me rephrase that," as the play continues with dynamic skating and waving. After an initial "No goal" call, the announcer excitedly exclaims, "Oh! And a goal!" as Louie scores, confirmed by the on-screen text 'GOAL by Louie, West 1 - East 0'. The announcer then playfully jabs, "He's doing a better job than Dallas. Thank you, Dallas," amidst loud crowd cheers. Confetti rains down, enhancing the festive atmosphere as the mascots, including Mick E. Moose, Gnash, Hunter, SJ Sharkie, Bailey, Buoy, Chance, Tommy Hawk, Sabretooth, Sparky, Iceburgh, Slapshot, Spartacat, Blades, NJ Devil, Stormy, Youppi!, Stinger, Gritty, Thunderbug, and Stanley C. Panther, converge in a celebratory group huddle near the center of the rink, performing slides, high-kicks, and waves. The announcer confirms the score, "One nothing." Overhead screens display animated mascot graphics, while photographers capture the joyful, theatrical event.

A referee in a black-and-white striped shirt oversees the game, while an overhead screen displays animated mascot graphics. The announcer highlights a "shot from downtown" and reminds everyone that "there is no icing" in mascot street hockey. The action intensifies as the announcer notes "NJ Devil behind the net," with the puck going into the Western Conference offensive zone, and Gritty "trying to hustle." Suddenly, SJ Sharkie scores a goal, prompting the announcer to exclaim, "And SJ Sharkie scores!" The overhead screen confirms the score: 'GOAL by SJ Sharkie, West 2 - East 0', with the announcer adding, "Not even 90 seconds in, and the Western Conference is up two to nothing." Following the goal, the mascots, including Hunter, Bailey, Sparky, Chance, Iceburgh, Slapshot, Spartacat, Blades, NJ Devil, Stormy, Youppi!, Stinger, Gritty, Thunderbug, Stanley C. segments.integrated_caption: The lively All-Star Mascot Competition unfolds on a vibrant blue indoor rink, branded with 'COLLECT THE BEST' and 'All-Star' logos, as an announcer sets the scene for the "Western Conference" action, exclaiming "Here we go!" Multiple NHL mascots, dressed in their team jerseys, engage in a chaotic game of floor hockey. Harvey the Hound, in a Calgary Flames jersey, is noted by the announcer as having "the ball," but Sparky, the blue and orange fox mascot, makes a "Great stop!" eliciting a collective "Oh!" from the crowd. The announcer then observes that "He's tough, that takes out Gritty though," referring to the orange, furry Gritty mascot, prompting the crowd to chant "Let's go Gritty!" The announcer reiterates Gritty's toughness, stating, "We're not sure what he's made of, but whatever he is, he's tough." After a shot from Harvey the Hound is "a little off the mark," the announcer soon declares "Another goal!" as Harvey scores, triggering a celebratory on-screen graphic: 'GOAL by Harvey the Hound, West 3 - East 0'. The announcer confirms the score, "Three nothing Western Conference!" The mascots then converge in a group huddle near the center of the rink, engaging in animated gestures. The announcer notes that "Gritty needs a breather" as the game is "about two and a half minutes in," with the Western Conference still leading "three nothing." He then challenges the "Eastern Conference looking to get a goal here," urging fans to "make some noise if you're an Eastern Conference fan!" The crowd responds with loud cheers, as the announcer concludes, "If you're an Eastern Conference fan, your team is down three nothing." The sequence ends with the mascots still gathered, celebrating under the bright arena lights.

segments.integrated_caption: The lively All-Star Mascot Competition unfolds on a vibrant blue indoor rink, scattered with confetti and branded with 'COLLECT THE BEST' and 'All-Star' logos. An announcer sets the scene, noting a "0-0 goal" and that the "Western Conference [is] up one" about "five minutes through this first period." A diverse group of mascots, including Youppi!, Harvey the Hound, Louie, Gritty, and others, engage in a playful floor hockey game with small yellow balls and sticks. A human referee in black-and-white stripes oversees the action. The crowd behind the barriers cheers enthusiastically, with distinct shouts of "Go, Gritty!" heard twice. A staff member instructs, "Guys, don't lean against the boards, please." The announcer describes the action, noting the "NJ Devil" trying to clear the puck, leading to a "scrum." A "great clear by Louie from the St. Louis Blues" is highlighted, with the announcer identifying him as "Louie, the 2020 NHL Mascot of the Year," living "up to his name." The announcer also praises "outstanding work by Victor E. Green" and an "otherworldly" save, later mentioning "Buddy the Puffin now in net" and "Corey the Coyote." Mascots like Gritty, the orange monster, slide and wave energetically, while Louie, the blue bear, skates and scores. Harvey the Hound, the white dog, also scores. Slapshot, the red, white, and blue eagle, performs a backflip. The overhead screen displays animated graphics, enhancing the spectacle. As the game progresses, the mascots converge in a celebratory group huddle, showcasing camaraderie and energy under the brightly lit environment adorned with banners and flags.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens on a vibrant, confetti-strewn blue indoor rink, branded with 'COLLECT THE BEST' and 'All-Star' logos, where NHL team mascots engage in a lively hockey game. Spartacat, an orange-haired cat in an Ottawa Senators jersey, scores a goal, shifting the on-screen graphic to West 3 - East 2. An announcer's voice then confirms the score, stating, "It's three to two in favor of the Western Conference," with "four minutes left in this first period," noting a "mental error" that reduced the lead. Mascots like Gnash, Mick E. Moose, Louie, Youppi!, and Stormy are seen skating, waving, dancing, and high-kicking, while a referee mascot oversees the action. The announcer actively tries to "get the crowd fired up," and the animated crowd behind the barriers responds with enthusiastic cheers and applause. Building anticipation, the announcer exclaims, "Oh, it's in! And we're tied at three!" as Iceburgh, the black and yellow penguin in a Pittsburgh Penguins jersey, scores a dramatic tying goal. The announcer enthusiastically identifies the scorer, asking, "Who else but Iceburgh? What a shot!" and referring to him as "the penguin from the Steel City" who has "tied this game at three apiece." The overhead screen displays animated graphics throughout the spectacle. As the announcer updates that there's "a little less than three and a half minutes left," the mascots gather in a celebratory group huddle at the center of the rink, concluding the segment amidst continued crowd excitement.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens on a vibrant, confetti-strewn blue indoor rink, branded with 'All-Star' and 'COLLECT THE BEST' logos, where a lively mascot hockey game is underway. An announcer's voice immediately sets the scene, commenting on the game, "We don't need any sticklers," and noting the score, "It's still 4-3 in favor of the Western Conference." Mascots like Harvey the Hound, Nordy, Fin, Victor E. Green, Howler, Mick E. Moose, Gnash, Hunter, SJ Sharkie, Bailey, Buoy, Chance, Louie, Tommy Hawk, Sabretooth, Sparky, Iceburgh, Slapshot, Spartacat, Blades, NJ Devil, Stormy, Youppi!, Stinger, Gritty, Thunderbug, Stanley C. Panther, and Chester the Cheetah, all in their NHL team jerseys, engage in playful hockey actions, skating, passing, and scoring goals with oversized sticks. A referee mascot in black-and-white stripes actively oversees the game. The announcer continues to update the time, stating, "One minute remaining in this first period. Less than one minute," and later, "All right, we're down to 35 seconds," and finally, "30 seconds to go," while reiterating the score, "Western Conference still up 4-3." Throughout, upbeat background music plays as the crowd behind the barriers cheers enthusiastically, with a distinct "Woo!" heard at one point, adding to the festive atmosphere. The mascots perform dynamic movements including slides, cartwheels, backflips, and high-kicks, showcasing their unique personalities. Overhead, a screen displays animated graphics, enhancing the spectacle. As the segment concludes, the mascots gather in a group huddle near the center of the rink, celebrating together, while photographers capture the event from the rink’s edge.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens on a vibrant, confetti-strewn 'All-Star' branded blue indoor rink, where various NHL mascots, including Stanley C. As the countdown concludes, the announcer declares, "End of the period!" to a burst of enthusiastic cheering from the crowd spectators. A scoreboard graphic appears, confirming the end of the first period. The announcer then clarifies, "That goal does not count," before announcing the score: "After the first period, the score is West 4, East 3," which is met with more cheers. Mascots like Victor E. Green and a Referee Mascot are seen on the rink, while photographers capture the event. The announcer continues, reminding everyone to visit the "Updeck NHL Mascot Club after today's game" to collect game items, favorite cards, and even get a trading card made of themselves, praising the "amazing things" happening there. Meanwhile, the mascots, including Gritty, Thunderbug, NJ Devil, and Sparky, gather in a large, celebratory huddle near the center of the rink, waving, dancing, and performing energetic moves like slides, high-kicks, and backflips, all under an overhead screen displaying animated mascot graphics, as the energetic music fades back in.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens on a lively, chaotic mascot hockey game on a blue indoor rink, branded with 'COLLECT THE BEST' and 'All-Star' logos, as announcers react with exclamations like "Oh, there's a good one!" and suggest getting an autograph. Amidst loud cheers and applause, Spartacat, an orange-haired cat mascot in an Ottawa Senators jersey, scores a goal, confirmed by the on-screen text 'GOAL by Spartacat, West 4 - East 5' and the announcer exclaiming, "And there he is! I think he just scored a goal! It's 5 to 4 Eastern Conference! They've taken the lead!" Confetti is scattered across the rink, enhancing the celebratory atmosphere as the Eastern Conference is repeatedly announced as having taken the lead. Following the goal, the mascots, including Howler, NJ Devil, and Gritty, disperse across the rink with dynamic movements, sliding, waving, and skating, while the Referee Mascot, an orange and black striped figure, interacts with players and moves toward the group. A staff member in a blue shirt also walks onto the rink, observing the mascots. The overhead screen displays animated mascot graphics, and photographers capture the event. The scene culminates in a group huddle near the center of the rink, where the mascots gather closely, some raising their sticks in celebration, with the Referee Mascot and staff member standing nearby, all under bright, consistent lighting, as the announcers continue to comment on the ongoing game, mentioning other mascots like "Iceberg" and "Thunderball" and discussing five-on-five play.

The announcer's excitement builds with exclamations like "My god, be careful out there!" and later, "Here comes Blades! It's a breakaway! Great opportunity! Oh!" leading to a loud cheer from the crowd. A Staff Member briefly enters the rink, interacting with the Referee Mascot amidst the ongoing playful collisions and acrobatic moves, as the announcer concludes, "That was what we needed!"

The mascots, including Blades (brown bear in Bruins jersey), Sparky (blue fox in Islanders jersey), Iceburgh (black penguin in Penguins jersey), Louie (blue bear in Blues jersey), and Stinger (green alien in Blue Jackets jersey), are seen skating, sliding, and attempting to score goals with small hockey sticks and a yellow ball. The action is fast-paced and comedic, with mascots colliding, falling, and performing acrobatic moves like slides and cartwheels. A white netted goal structure is positioned on the right side, and a Referee Mascot in a striped outfit actively oversees the game, blowing a whistle and gesturing. The crowd of spectators behind barriers cheers enthusiastically, adding to the festive atmosphere. The Overhead Screen displays animated graphics, enhancing the event's spectacle. As the game progresses, the mascots converge near the goal, engaging in a group huddle, with some like Gritty (orange monster in Flyers jersey) and Stanley C. Panther (tan panther in Panthers jersey) joining in with energetic gestures. A Staff Member in a blue shirt briefly enters the rink to interact with the Referee Mascot, possibly to manage the event. The overall mood is celebratory and entertaining, with mascots showcasing their unique personalities through dynamic movements and playful interactions.

segments.integrated_caption: The All-Star Mascot Competition unfolds on a vibrant blue indoor rink, scattered with colorful confetti, set to upbeat electronic music and the enthusiastic roars of the crowd. Announcers provide dynamic commentary, initially reacting with a surprised "No. No." to an early play and noting that the "Western Conference [is] still in the lead." Mascots in NHL jerseys, including Stormy (Carolina Hurricanes), Howler (Arizona Coyotes), and Buoy (Seattle Kraken), actively skate and play floor hockey. The announcer highlights the "NJ Devil" attempting a shot, exclaiming "Look out! Look out!" as the action intensifies. A pivotal moment arrives when Stormy takes a shot, prompting the announcer's excited call, "Stormy with a shot! And a goal! And a goal!" On-screen text confirms 'GOAL by Stormy, West 7 - East 7', with the announcer adding, "Stormy from the Carolina Hurricanes has... I just gave Pat seven," and later confirming, "We're tied at seven." The Referee Mascot oversees the game, occasionally blowing a whistle, while other mascots like Blades (Boston Bruins) perform energetic slides and high-kicks, and Slapshot (Washington Capitals) executes a backflip. The announcer directly engages the "Crowd Spectators," asking, "Are you guys having a good time so far?" to which they respond with a roaring cheer, followed by another encouraged "Let me hear you make some noise for the mascots!" and an even louder crowd response. As the announcer repeatedly emphasizes, "Time is ticking away," the mascots converge in a group huddle, waving and celebrating together, showcasing camaraderie and the vibrant spirit of the event, all under the watchful eye of the overhead screen displaying animated graphics.

segments.integrated_caption: The lively All-Star Mascot Competition unfolds on a vibrant blue indoor rink, scattered with colorful confetti and marked with red boundary lines, as a large crowd cheers behind barriers under banners and an overhead screen displaying animated graphics. An announcer encourages the crowd, instructing them to "start stomping your feet until somebody scores a goal," which they enthusiastically do, creating a "little rumble" in the arena. Mascots like Gritty, Blades, and Youppi!, dressed in their NHL team jerseys, actively engage in a playful game of floor hockey, maneuvering a small ball with sticks toward a netted goal. The announcer notes the score is "tied at seven" before a horn blares and the crowd erupts in cheers as a "GOAL!" is scored. On-screen text confirms this is a 'GOAL by Blades, West 7 - East 8', prompting the announcer to exclaim, "Oh, it actually worked!" and comment on Blades, "He's gonna come for us." The game continues with the announcer then guiding the crowd to "start slow clapping until somebody scores a goal," which they follow, creating a rhythmic sound. Another "GOAL!" is announced with a horn and renewed crowd excitement, with the announcer declaring, "You did it again, fans!" and noting the score update, "He's gonna take the lead!" and "It makes it seven." Following this, the mascots transition into a dynamic group huddle, moving across the rink with exaggerated gestures, slides, and waves. Gritty slides on his knees, Youppi! dances with arms raised, and Sabretooth performs a high-kick, while the Referee Mascot oversees and then joins the celebratory group, which also includes Sparky, Stormy, and Stanley C. Panther. The rink's boards display 'COLLECT THE BEST' and 'All-Star' branding, reinforcing the festive and energetic atmosphere, as the sequence concludes with the mascots gathered centrally, waving and interacting with the cheering crowd amidst the scattered confetti.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens on a vibrant blue indoor rink, branded with 'COLLECT THE BEST' and 'All-Star' logos, where a lively mascot hockey game is underway, accompanied by upbeat, energetic rock/pop music and constant crowd cheering. An enthusiastic announcer provides play-by-play, urging on the action. Mascots in colorful team jerseys, like Gnash, a saber-toothed tiger in a Nashville Predators jersey, who the announcer cheers with 'Go, Gnash! Let's go!', skate dynamically. Other mascots, including Sparky, a fox in a New York Islanders jersey, are actively involved, with the announcer noting a blocked scoring chance for Sparky. The game is intense, with the announcer repeatedly stating, 'We're tied at eight!' and giving a time update: 'You've got about five minutes left in this second period.' Bernie, a white bear-like mascot, makes a 'great block,' while Victor E. Green, a green alien-like mascot, 'clears that' according to the announcer. Iceburgh, a penguin mascot, attempts a 'backhand' shot. The festive atmosphere is enhanced by confetti scattered across the rink and a large cheering crowd. The action culminates in a goal scored by Nordy, an orange bear mascot, which the announcer excitedly declares as 'Nordy with the game-winner!' On-screen text confirms 'GOAL by Nordy, West 9 - East 8'. Following the goal, a large group of mascots, including Harvey the Hound, Bernie, Fin, Victor E. Green, Howler, Mick E. Moose, Gnash, Hunter, SJ Sharkie, Bailey, Buoy, Chance, Louie, Tommy Hawk, Sabretooth, Sparky, Iceburgh, Slapshot, Spartacat, Blades, NJ Devil, Stormy, Youppi!, Stinger, Gritty, Thunderbug, Stanley C. Panther, and Chester the Cheetah, along with the Referee Mascot, gather in a celebratory huddle. The overhead screen displays animated mascot graphics as the mascots perform dynamic moves like slides, cartwheels, backflips, and high-kicks, before waving and celebrating with the crowd, bringing the entertaining event to a joyful finale.

segments.integrated_caption: The lively All-Star Mascot Competition unfolds on a blue indoor rink, branded with 'COLLECT THE BEST' and 'All-Star' logos, as an energetic announcer declares, "two minutes to go!" and updates the score, "9 to 8 in favor of the Western Conference." Mascots like Sparky, Iceburgh, and Blades skate and jostle for position, while the announcer encourages the "large crowd of spectators" to "get some clapping going," which they respond to with cheers and applause. Confetti is scattered across the rink, adding to the festive atmosphere. The game intensifies, with the announcer building excitement, leading to a dramatic moment where Youppi! scores a goal, visually confirmed by the on-screen text 'GOAL by Youppi! West 9 - East 9'. The announcer exclaims, "YES! That's it!" amidst a loud cheer and a goal sound effect, then clarifies, "We've tied this at 9." The mascots regroup, performing dynamic moves like slides and high-kicks, with Gritty and Stinger engaging in playful interactions. The Referee Mascot oversees the game, occasionally blowing a whistle. The Overhead Screen displays animated graphics. The announcer humorously remarks, "Of all the athletic events you've seen in your lifetime, this is one of them," before asking the crowd, "who wants the Eastern Conference to win?" as the mascots converge into a group huddle, waving and striking poses. A Staff Member briefly enters the rink to interact with the Referee Mascot before stepping back, all under bright, consistent lighting that highlights the vibrant costumes and energetic movements, creating a joyful and entertaining event.

segments.integrated_caption: The lively All-Star Mascot Competition unfolds on a vibrant blue indoor rink, scattered with colorful confetti, as NHL team mascots engage in a chaotic game of floor hockey. The segment opens with loud crowd cheers and a whistle, as an announcer declares the score is 'Nine at nine' and 'it's anyone's game' with 'three minutes left.' Upbeat, energetic background music with a driving beat begins, accompanying the mascots' exaggerated movements—sliding, waving, and skating—while they maneuver a small yellow ball with sticks. The referee mascot in black-and-white stripes actively oversees the game, with their whistle audibly blowing. The announcer continues to provide commentary, noting the mascots 'trying to find that ball' and urging them to 'start passing.' The crowd behind the 'COLLECT THE BEST' and 'All-Star' branded boards cheers enthusiastically throughout. The announcer identifies Youppi! dancing and waving, then calls out Stinger 'looking to score.' A goal is scored by Blades, a brown bear mascot, as an on-screen graphic confirms 'GOAL by Blades, West 9 - East 10.' This moment is met with a massive, enthusiastic 'WOO!' from the crowd and roaring cheers, as the announcer exclaims, 'Great save! Great... Ten to nine, it's favored for the Eastern Conference!' The mascots then converge in a celebratory group huddle near the center of the rink, performing dynamic gestures like high-kicks and slides, all under the bright lighting and animated graphics of the overhead screen, as the crowd's excitement continues.

segments.integrated_caption: The lively mascot hockey game unfolds on a blue indoor rink, branded with 'COLLECT THE BEST' and 'All-Star' logos, as an announcer declares "under two minutes to go" and a score of "10-9" in favor of the Eastern Conference. Upbeat music plays as mascots like Stanley C. Panther and Blades engage in a face-off near center ice, overseen by the Referee Mascot. The announcer emphasizes the Eastern Conference's lead, stating they are "two minutes away from winning this," while the Western Conference is urged to keep it a one-goal game. Dynamic skating, sliding, and goal attempts continue, with the announcer commenting on a play where "SJ" (possibly Sparky) tries to score, and explicitly mentioning "You see Blades trying to get that goal." The tension builds, and then a loud "Goal!" from the announcer is met with enthusiastic crowd cheers as Blades scores, changing the on-screen text to 'GOAL by Blades, West 9 - East 11'. The announcer confirms, "He's getting another goal! It's 11-9!" Mascots then converge in a celebratory group huddle near the goal, with Gritty sliding on his knees, Youppi! waving, and Stormy skating in. segments.integrated_caption: The All-Star Mascot Competition Rink is a vibrant scene of celebration, with a large group of mascots, including Victor E. Green, Harvey the Hound, Nordy, Fin, Howler, Mick E. Moose, Gnash, Hunter, SJ Sharkie, Bailey, Buoy, Chance, Louie, Tommy Hawk, Sabretooth, Sparky, Iceburgh, Slapshot, Spartacat, Blades, NJ Devil, Stormy, Youppi!, Stinger, Gritty, Thunderbug, Stanley C. Panther, and Chester the Cheetah, tightly huddled together amidst scattered confetti. Upbeat, celebratory music plays as an announcer's voice, accompanied by enthusiastic crowd cheers, introduces Amanda Whitaker Smith from Upper Deck, who is presenting Carlton, the bear mascot in a Montréal Canadiens jersey, with his very own Upper Deck trading card. The announcer praises Upper Deck for their "great playing service" and encourages spectators to visit the "upperdecknhlmascotclub.clubhouse" for trading cards and cool games, even mentioning the possibility of getting a "trading card made on yourself." As the Referee Mascot, whistle in hand, engages with the group and a Staff Member approaches, the announcer declares that Carlton will receive even more. From Chester the Cheetah, the orange and black cheetah mascot, Carlton is presented with "the biggest bag of puffy Cheetos I've ever seen in my life," eliciting more cheers and laughter from the crowd. The announcer then directs Carlton to "turn that bag around so we can see it" and "raise it high as if it were the Stanley Cup," a command met with further enthusiastic crowd reactions, reinforcing the festive and celebratory atmosphere of the event. Photographers remain crouched at the rink's edge, capturing every moment under the bright overhead lights, while the 'COLLECT THE BEST' and 'All-Star' branding on the boards highlights the event’s theme.
